steps.title: Identify the tense frame
steps.content: The passage establishes a past-tense narrative: events are anchored to 1987 and described in simple past. The blank appears in the sentence "Years later, he ______ for this groundbreaking research with the Robert A. Welch Award" — a future event described from within that past narrative frame.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "would be honored": The conditional/future-in-the-past form. "Would be honored" correctly describes an event that was in the future at the time of the 1987 starting point but has since occurred — fitting the past-tense narrative. ✓
  • "is honored": Simple present tense clashes with the past-tense narrative frame established throughout the passage. ✗
  • "will be honored": Simple future is wrong because the honoring is presented as already having happened; simple future implies it has not yet occurred. ✗
  • "had been honored": Past perfect places the honoring before 1987, contradicting "Years later" — the award came after the research, not before. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
